pass1_value.note: The Asana API is RESTful (base URL https://app.asana.com/api/1.0) and documents endpoints for tasks, projects, users, teams, webhooks, attachments, custom fields, etc., covering most product objects.

final_value.note: The official API reference states the Asana API is organized around REST, uses resource-oriented URLs, accepts/returns JSON, and uses standard HTTP verbs/status codes, with base URL https://app.asana.com/api/1.0. The page enumerates many resources (e.g., tasks, projects, teams, users, webhooks, attachments, custom fields), indicating broad coverage.

pass1_value.note: Public REST API docs plus self-serve personal access tokens and app registration in the developer console allow building integrations today.

final_value.note: Asana's publicly accessible API reference documents a REST API (base URL https://app.asana.com/api/1.0) and the authentication docs specify OAuth2 and personal access tokens, supporting that integrations can be built today.
